title: Lucerne, Interlaken & Zurich.

trains.name: Trains & Taxi Transfers
trains.blurb: Scenic Swiss rail travel linking Lucerne, Interlaken and Zurich, with the Swiss Travel Pass covering transfers and a Mount Titlis excursion.

transport.1: Arrival transfer by train from Zurich Airport to Lucerne.
transport.2: Full-day excursion to Mount Titlis by cogwheel railway and rotating cable car, plus a Lake Lucerne cruise — both included with the 4-day Swiss Travel Pass.
transport.3: Lucerne to Interlaken by train, II Class, using the Swiss Travel Pass.
transport.5: Interlaken to Zurich by train, II Class, using the Swiss Travel Pass.
transport.6: Departure transfer from Zurich hotel to Zurich Airport.

inclusions: Swiss Travel Pass, 4 consecutive days, II Class
Mount Titlis excursion with cogwheel railway and cable car
Lake Lucerne cruise, free with the Swiss Travel Pass
Lucerne to Interlaken train transfer, II Class
Interlaken to Zurich train transfer, II Class

days.title: Arrive Lucerne
days.desc: Upon arrival in Switzerland, utilize the Swiss Travel Pass for your onward journey to Lucerne. The Pass covers seamless travel across the Swiss Travel System, enjoying unlimited travel on trains, boats, trams, and buses. Overnight in Lucerne.
days.taste: Confiserie Bachmann, a Lucerne institution since 1938, for Luzerner Lebkuchen — the city's spiced honey gingerbread — with a coffee on the Kapellplatz.
days.shopping: Lucerne's Old Town arcades are known for Swiss watches and chocolate — Bucherer and local chocolatiers line the Kapellplatz.

days.title: Mount Titlis excursion
days.desc: Utilize your Swiss Travel Pass to explore Mount Titlis, a breathtaking alpine experience. Revel in the stunning vistas, engage in snow activities, and relish the revolving Titlis Rotair gondola. Later, you can take a cruise on Lake Lucerne, enjoying the serene waters and picturesque surroundings. Overnight in Lucerne.
days.experience: The Titlis Cliff Walk, Europe's highest suspension bridge, crosses a ridge just below the summit station.
days.taste: A rösti with alpine cheese and speck at one of the mountain restaurants around Trübsee — the valley's standard lunch after a morning on the glacier.

days.title: Lucerne → Interlaken
days.desc: Embark on a scenic train journey from Lucerne to Interlaken, reveling in the breathtaking vistas along the way. As you arrive, experience the allure of Interlaken, nestled between Lake Thun and Lake Brienz, offering a gateway to the Bernese Oberland's splendour. Overnight in Interlaken.

days.title: Interlaken at leisure
days.desc: Enjoy the freedom to explore Switzerland at your leisure using the Swiss Travel Pass. Delve into the scenic wonders of Grindelwald and Interlaken, known for their stunning landscapes. Optionally, consider a visit to Grindelwald First for panoramic views and thrilling activities. Overnight in Interlaken.
days.experience: Interlaken is one of the world's best-known paragliding hubs — tandem flights launch from Beatenberg or Schilthorn and land in the Höhematte park in the town centre.
days.taste: Rugenbräu, brewed in nearby Matten since 1885, is the local beer worth ordering in Interlaken's bars and restaurants.
days.fallbackIncluded: Day at leisure — optional Jungfraujoch 'Top of Europe' excursion available.

days.title: Interlaken → Zurich
days.desc: Travel from Interlaken to Zurich using your Swiss Travel Pass, exploring the vibrant city of Zurich upon arrival. Zurich, a financial hub, blends modernity with history, offering a plethora of cultural attractions. Optionally, venture further to Schaffhausen by train to witness the majestic Rhine Falls, Europe's largest waterfall. Overnight Zurich.
days.taste: Zürcher Geschnetzeltes — veal in white wine and cream sauce with rösti — is Zurich's signature dish, served at the Kronenhalle, the artists' restaurant on Rämistrasse that's been open since 1924.
days.shopping: Bahnhofstrasse is one of the world's most exclusive shopping avenues, lined with flagship boutiques and Swiss watchmakers.

days.title: Depart Zurich
days.desc: Make your way to Zurich Airport from the city center. As your memorable Swiss adventure concludes, cherish the scenic journey and bid adieu to the beautiful landscapes and hospitable culture of Switzerland.

about.title: Best season
about.body: April–October for the best weather and full alpine access; December–March suits winter and ski travellers.

about.title: Weather
about.body: Alpine climate with warm summers (15–25°C) and cold, snowy winters, especially at altitude on Mount Titlis.

about.title: Currency & docs
about.body: Switzerland's local currency is the Swiss Franc (CHF), though this package is priced in Euro; it is not an EU member but is part of the Schengen Area, so no separate visa is needed for Schengen visitors.

goodToKnow.title: Tickets & timing
goodToKnow.body: Mount Titlis cable cars are busiest at midday in summer — an early start helps avoid queues at Engelberg.

goodToKnow.title: Rail travel
goodToKnow.body: Swiss trains run frequently and punctually; the included Swiss Travel Pass covers all rail travel and the Lake Lucerne cruise on this itinerary.

goodToKnow.title: What to pack
goodToKnow.body: Bring warm layers and sturdy shoes for the mountain excursion, plus sunglasses for glare on the Titlis glacier.

goodToKnow.title: Altitude
goodToKnow.body: Mount Titlis summit sits above 3,000 metres — dress warmly and move at an easy pace if you're not used to altitude.

goodToKnow.title: Money
goodToKnow.body: Cards are accepted almost everywhere in Switzerland, including mountain kiosks on Titlis; a little Swiss Franc cash is still useful for market stalls and public toilets.

terms: All rates are net and per person for the package.
Child rates apply for children aged 2 to 11 years old. One child sharing a room with 2 adults, subject to hotel policy.
All rates are subject to availability at the time of booking. Europe Incoming will endeavour to match the rates quoted.
Rates are not applicable during trade fair periods, major European public holidays, and other major events.
City taxes are not included in the price.
If certain attractions are closed during specific periods, alternative options will be arranged.
All bookings must be confirmed at least 60 working days prior to arrival.
100% pre-payment required by bank transfer or credit card (Visa or Mastercard). All bank transfer charges covered by the Agent.
Vouchers will be issued after receipt of full payment.
